events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the assessment JSON directly to `/app/answer.json`, treating the wrong datum reference during gauging as a non-compliant inspection condition at rough-complete stage.

events.label: assistant
events.text: I have the workspace confirmed. The file will contain only valid JSON, with the defect cues focused on datum scheme misuse and resulting invalid acceptance/checkoff.

events.label: assistant
events.text: The JSON file is in place. I’ll do a quick parse check so there isn’t a formatting issue.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc 'python -m json.tool /app/answer.json'
→ {
    "decision": "fail",
    "risk": "high",
    "work_stage": "rough-complete",

        "installed-defective",
        "non-compliant"
    ],
    "percent_complete": 70,
    "value": 0,
    "sound_source": "",

    "rate": 0,
    "order": [],

        "Part was checked off the wrong surface instead of the specified datum",
        "Gauge result and acceptance status are not valid for the GD&T requirement",
        "Installed or assembled part is non-compliant until rechecked against the correct datum reference frame"
    ],…
